questions.prompt: Backups and response plans haven’t been reviewed recently. What should be done?

options.label: A
options.text: Review backup availability and test recovery process

options.label: B
options.text: Assume backups work because they ran last year

options.label: C
options.text: Ignore unless an incident occurs

questions.prompt: A warning is issued about ransomware targeting your industry. What is the best action?

options.label: A
options.text: Inform IT/security and check protections immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Wait to see if attacks increase first

options.label: C
options.text: Assume antivirus will handle it

questions.prompt: Staff training on ransomware is scheduled. Why is this important?

options.label: A
options.text: It helps staff recognise suspicious behaviour early

options.label: B
options.text: It is mainly a compliance exercise

options.label: C
options.text: Staff do not need training on ransomware

questions.prompt: Your organisation is considering external investigation support. What is the key benefit?

options.label: A
options.text: Faster response and expert guidance during an incident

options.label: B
options.text: Only useful after systems fail

options.label: C
options.text: Not necessary unless an attack happens first

questions.prompt: A user reports files suddenly cannot be opened. What should the service desk do?

options.label: A
options.text: Log the incident and escalate immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Ask the user to restart their computer

options.label: C
options.text: Ignore unless more reports appear

questions.prompt: Several machines show ransom messages. What should happen?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the incident response team immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Wait to see if systems recover themselves

options.label: C
options.text: Let local IT handle it alone

questions.prompt: One department is affected. What should be checked first?

options.label: A
options.text: Whether other systems or shared drives are impacted

options.label: B
options.text: Only fix the first machine

options.label: C
options.text: Assume the issue is isolated

questions.prompt: Early response begins. What information is most important to collect?

options.label: A
options.text: When it started, what systems are affected, and what users saw

options.label: B
options.text: Only screenshots of ransom messages

options.label: C
options.text: Skip data collection and focus only on fixing systems

questions.prompt: Ransomware is confirmed on multiple systems. What should happen?

options.label: A
options.text: Escalate according to incident response procedures

options.label: B
options.text: Handle it quietly within the department

options.label: C
options.text: Avoid escalation to reduce concern

questions.prompt: Investigation may be required. What should staff be told?

options.label: A
options.text: Leave affected systems as they are and follow instructions

options.label: B
options.text: Restart computers to try fixing the issue

options.label: C
options.text: Delete suspicious files themselves

questions.prompt: The scale of the attack is unclear. What should happen?

options.label: A
options.text: Involve IT/security specialists immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Ask general staff to troubleshoot

options.label: C
options.text: Delay involvement until systems fail completely

questions.prompt: IT wants to identify the ransomware type. Why is this useful?

options.label: A
options.text: It helps determine recovery options and risk level

options.label: B
options.text: Only needed for technical curiosity

options.label: C
options.text: Not necessary for response

questions.prompt: Files across shared drives are encrypted. What must be assessed?

options.label: A
options.text: Which departments, systems, and data are affected

options.label: B
options.text: Only the first device reported

options.label: C
options.text: Treat as a single-user issue

questions.prompt: If IT says they need to analyse a suspicious file, what should staff do?

options.label: A
options.text: Send it only through the official reporting channel

options.label: B
options.text: Forward to colleagues to ask opinions

options.label: C
options.text: Try opening it on another device

questions.prompt: Sensitive business files may be affected. What is critical now?

options.label: A
options.text: Confirm which systems and data are impacted

options.label: B
options.text: Focus only on restoring computers

options.label: C
options.text: Assume all data is equally affected

questions.prompt: There is a risk customer data may be exposed. What is safest?

options.label: A
options.text: Escalate to the data protection lead for assessment

options.label: B
options.text: Monitor quietly first

options.label: C
options.text: Avoid reporting to protect reputation

questions.prompt: Encryption activity is still happening. What should be done?

options.label: A
options.text: Isolate affected machines immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Wait to see if it stops on its own

options.label: C
options.text: Keep systems running to avoid disruption

questions.prompt: Suspicious logins are detected. Why suspend accounts?

options.label: A
options.text: To stop attackers from continuing access

options.label: B
options.text: Only to follow procedure

options.label: C
options.text: Not needed unless systems fail again

questions.prompt: IT begins system clean-up. What is important?

options.label: A
options.text: Ensure all traces of ransomware are removed safely

options.label: B
options.text: Delete obvious infected files only

options.label: C
options.text: Resume work immediately

questions.prompt: Recovery planning begins. Why restore from trusted backups?

options.label: A
options.text: It ensures systems return without hidden infection

options.label: B
options.text: It’s just the fastest way to recover

options.label: C
options.text: Not necessary if systems start working again

questions.prompt: IT proposes rebuilding machines completely. Is this necessary?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes, to confirm no hidden compromise remains

options.label: B
options.text: Only if users complain again

options.label: C
options.text: No, once files return, everything is safe

questions.prompt: Systems appear stable again. How should recovery proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Restore gradually and confirm everything is safe

options.label: B
options.text: Turn everything back on immediately

options.label: C
options.text: Let departments restart systems themselves

questions.prompt: The incident is resolved. What is the main purpose of the report?

options.label: A
options.text: Document what happened and improve future response

options.label: B
options.text: Only record who was responsible

options.label: C
options.text: Avoid writing one to reduce visibility

questions.prompt: How should the organisation use this incident?

options.label: A
options.text: Review gaps and improve controls and training

options.label: B
options.text: Discuss informally only

options.label: C
options.text: Move on without review

questions.prompt: Employees know about the incident. What is best?

options.label: A
options.text: Share guidance to help prevent future ransomware attacks

options.label: B
options.text: Tell only management

options.label: C
options.text: Avoid discussing it

questions.prompt: Weaknesses in procedures were identified. What now?

options.label: A
options.text: Update response plans and security controls formally

options.label: B
options.text: Make small notes only

options.label: C
options.text: Leave documentation unchanged
